{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Fleet Manager",
  "description": "Jamub Group of Companies is a diversified Nigerian conglomerate with active operations in construction, engineering, fintech, Oil &amp; Gas, Fast-Moving Consumer Goods (FMCG), and agribusiness. Our Architecture, Engineering &amp; Construction (AEC) division manages a growing portfolio of residential estates, institutional buildings, industrial parks, and road infrastructure projects across multiple states.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Fleet Manager

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

&nbsp;

Job Summary


	The Fleet Manager is responsible for the end-to-end management of Jamub Group&#39;s construction fleet, comprising heavy-duty trucks, concrete mixers, excavators, bulldozers, graders, cranes, tippers, and all other plant and equipment deployed across the Group&#39;s active project sites.
	The role demands a technically competent, operationally disciplined professional who can minimise fleet downtime, enforce preventive maintenance schedules, control fleet operating costs, maintain full regulatory compliance, and optimise equipment deployment across a multi-site construction portfolio.
	The Fleet Manager is the Group&#39;s principal guardian against equipment-related project delays and capital asset deterioration.


Key Responsibilties
Fleet Deployment &amp; Utilisation Management:


	Manage the day-to-day deployment of all trucks, heavy machinery, and plant equipment across active project sites.
	Maintain a master fleet deployment schedule aligned to project demands, eliminating idle time and ensuring all assets are optimally utilised.


Preventive Maintenance Programme:


	Design, implement, and strictly enforce a preventive maintenance programme for the entire fleet covering all heavy machinery and trucks.
	Ensure all scheduled servicing, lubrication, tyre changes, and component inspections are completed on time and fully documented.


Breakdown Management &amp; Downtime Reduction:


	Operate a rapid-response breakdown system to minimise equipment downtime at project sites. Maintain an approved register of mechanics and service contractors.
	Track mean-time-to-repair (MTTR) per equipment category and report all critical breakdowns to the Technical Director within 24 hours.


Fleet Cost Control &amp; Budget Management:


	Manage the fleet operations budget covering fuel, maintenance, spare parts, tyres, and third-party repairs
	Monitor cost-per-km and cost-per-machine-hour for all assets, identify overruns early, and present monthly cost performance reports to the Technical Director.


Fuel Management &amp; Consumption Control:


	Implement a rigorous fuel management system daily fuel logs, consumption benchmarks per equipment category, and anomaly reporting. Eliminate fuel wastage, unauthorised usage, and pilferage.
	Produce monthly fuel efficiency reports.


Regulatory Compliance &amp; Documentation:


	Ensure full regulatory compliance for all fleet assets valid registration, roadworthiness certificates, insurance, and operator licences for all vehicles and machinery.
	Maintain a compliance calendar and renew all documents before expiry.
	Conduct quarterly physical audits of all assets.


Fleet Performance Reporting:


	Produce weekly and monthly fleet performance reports covering utilisation rates, maintenance compliance, downtime incidents, fuel consumption, cost performance, and compliance status.
	Present reports to the Technical Director and contribute fleet data to PMO project dashboards.


Qualifications &amp; Requirements
Education:


	B.Sc / B.Tech / HND in Mechanical Engineering, Automobile Engineering, or Civil Engineering.


Professional Certification:


	NSE or COREN membership is preferred.
	CILT certification or equivalent in Fleet/Logistics/Plant Operations is a strong advantage.


Experience:


	Minimum of 8 years in fleet or plant management, with at least 4 years managing heavy construction equipment and commercial vehicles in a large construction or infrastructure company.


Technical Skills:


	Strong technical knowledge of heavy construction plant and commercial trucks. Proficiency in fleet management software and Microsoft Excel. Familiarity with fuel management systems is required.


Core Competencies &amp; Personal Attributes:


	Deep technical knowledge of heavy construction machinery, plant maintenance, and commercial vehicle operations.
	Strong operational discipline enforces maintenance schedules and compliance requirements without exception.
	Rigorous cost management tracks and controls fleet operating costs with precision.
	Effective people manager leads drivers, operators, and mechanics with clear expectations and accountability.
	Proactive problem-solver anticipates breakdowns and compliance risks before they impact project delivery.
	High integrity manages fuel, spare parts, and fleet assets with zero tolerance for pilferage or misuse.
	Strong reporting skills produce clear, accurate fleet performance reports for technical and executive audiences.
	Resilient under pressure, manages competing equipment demands across multiple sites without compromising standards.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary commensurate with experience and qualifications.
	Opportunity to manage a large, diverse construction fleet across a growing multi-state project portfolio.
	Professional development support
	A structured, performance-driven work environment with clear career progression pathways.
	Direct exposure to senior technical leadership and involvement in operational strategy decisions.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send CV and a brief cover letter &nbsp;to: jobs@jamubgroup.com copying hr@jamubgroup.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

Fleet Manager Job at Jamub Group of Companies




        
    

        
        Jamub Group of Companies is a diversified Nigerian conglomerate with active operations in construction, engineering, fintech, Oil & Gas, Fast-Moving Consumer Goods (FMCG), and agribusiness. Our Architecture, Engineering & Construction (AEC) division manages a growing portfolio of residential estates, institutional buildings, industrial parks, and road infrastructure projects across multiple states.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Fleet Manager

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

 

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Fleet Manager is responsible for the end-to-end management of Jamub Group's construction fleet, comprising heavy-duty trucks, concrete mixers, excavators, bulldozers, graders, cranes, tippers, and all other plant and equipment deployed across the Group's active project sites.
	The role demands a technically competent, operationally disciplined professional who can minimise fleet downtime, enforce preventive maintenance schedules, control fleet operating costs, maintain full regulatory compliance, and optimise equipment deployment across a multi-site construction portfolio.
	The Fleet Manager is the Group's principal guardian against equipment-related project delays and capital asset deterioration.


Key Responsibilties
Fleet Deployment & Utilisation Management:


	Manage the day-to-day deployment of all trucks, heavy machinery, and plant equipment across active project sites.
	Maintain a master fleet deployment schedule aligned to project demands, eliminating idle time and ensuring all assets are optimally utilised.


Preventive Maintenance Programme:


	Design, implement, and strictly enforce a preventive maintenance programme for the entire fleet covering all heavy machinery and trucks.
	Ensure all scheduled servicing, lubrication, tyre changes, and component inspections are completed on time and fully documented.


Breakdown Management & Downtime Reduction:


	Operate a rapid-response breakdown system to minimise equipment downtime at project sites. Maintain an approved register of mechanics and service contractors.
	Track mean-time-to-repair (MTTR) per equipment category and report all critical breakdowns to the Technical Director within 24 hours.


Fleet Cost Control & Budget Management:


	Manage the fleet operations budget covering fuel, maintenance, spare parts, tyres, and third-party repairs
	Monitor cost-per-km and cost-per-machine-hour for all assets, identify overruns early, and present monthly cost performance reports to the Technical Director.


Fuel Management & Consumption Control:


	Implement a rigorous fuel management system daily fuel logs, consumption benchmarks per equipment category, and anomaly reporting. Eliminate fuel wastage, unauthorised usage, and pilferage.
	Produce monthly fuel efficiency reports.


Regulatory Compliance & Documentation:


	Ensure full regulatory compliance for all fleet assets valid registration, roadworthiness certificates, insurance, and operator licences for all vehicles and machinery.
	Maintain a compliance calendar and renew all documents before expiry.
	Conduct quarterly physical audits of all assets.


Fleet Performance Reporting:


	Produce weekly and monthly fleet performance reports covering utilisation rates, maintenance compliance, downtime incidents, fuel consumption, cost performance, and compliance status.
	Present reports to the Technical Director and contribute fleet data to PMO project dashboards.


Qualifications & Requirements
Education:


	B.Sc / B.Tech / HND in Mechanical Engineering, Automobile Engineering, or Civil Engineering.


Professional Certification:


	NSE or COREN membership is preferred.
	CILT certification or equivalent in Fleet/Logistics/Plant Operations is a strong advantage.


Experience:


	Minimum of 8 years in fleet or plant management, with at least 4 years managing heavy construction equipment and commercial vehicles in a large construction or infrastructure company.


Technical Skills:


	Strong technical knowledge of heavy construction plant and commercial trucks. Proficiency in fleet management software and Microsoft Excel. Familiarity with fuel management systems is required.


Core Competencies & Personal Attributes:


	Deep technical knowledge of heavy construction machinery, plant maintenance, and commercial vehicle operations.
	Strong operational discipline enforces maintenance schedules and compliance requirements without exception.
	Rigorous cost management tracks and controls fleet operating costs with precision.
	Effective people manager leads drivers, operators, and mechanics with clear expectations and accountability.
	Proactive problem-solver anticipates breakdowns and compliance risks before they impact project delivery.
	High integrity manages fuel, spare parts, and fleet assets with zero tolerance for pilferage or misuse.
	Strong reporting skills produce clear, accurate fleet performance reports for technical and executive audiences.
	Resilient under pressure, manages competing equipment demands across multiple sites without compromising standards.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary commensurate with experience and qualifications.
	Opportunity to manage a large, diverse construction fleet across a growing multi-state project portfolio.
	Professional development support
	A structured, performance-driven work environment with clear career progression pathways.
	Direct exposure to senior technical leadership and involvement in operational strategy decisions.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send CV and a brief cover letter  to: [email protected] copying [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Capital Market Analyst",
  "description": "At Elvaridah, we emphasize on the critical matters and prospects of organizations such as business growth, strategy, planning, marketing, structure, and people management across different industries. We help companies focus on the growth of their businesses while aligning costs with strategy. With our all-inclusive approach and expertise, we capture values across the boundaries of any organization. Elvaridah focuses on optimizing all the different parts of a business as a unit.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Capital Market Analyst

Job Location: Lagos (Hybrid)
Department: Investment &amp; Capital Markets
Reports To: Head, Capital Markets
Employment Type: Full-Time

Job Summary


	The Capital Market Analyst will be responsible for conducting market research, analyzing investment opportunities, monitoring capital market trends, and providing data-driven insights to support investment decisions and capital raising activities.
	The role requires strong analytical capabilities, financial modelling expertise, and a deep understanding of Nigeria&#39;s capital markets and regulatory environment.


Key Responsibilities
Market Research &amp; Analysis:


	Conduct comprehensive research and analysis of capital market trends, economic indicators, and industry developments.
	Monitor equity, debt, and alternative investment markets and provide timely market intelligence.
	Analyze financial statements, company performance, and investment opportunities.
	Prepare market reports, investment recommendations, and sector analyses.


Financial Modelling &amp; Valuation:


	Develop financial models for company valuations, capital raising transactions, and investment analysis.
	Conduct business valuations using DCF, comparable companies, and precedent transaction methodologies.
	Evaluate Initial Public Offerings (IPOs), bond issuances, rights issues, and other capital market transactions.
	Support due diligence processes for investment and fundraising activities.


Investment Advisory &amp; Portfolio Support:


	Assist in developing investment strategies and recommendations.
	Monitor portfolio performance and provide periodic performance reports.
	Conduct risk assessments and scenario analyses.
	Support transaction execution and capital market deals.


Regulatory &amp; Compliance Monitoring:


	Stay updated on capital market regulations, policies, and compliance requirements.
	Ensure adherence to guidelines issued by regulatory authorities and market operators.
	Support the preparation of regulatory filings and transaction documentation.


Reporting &amp; Stakeholder Engagement:


	Prepare presentations and investment memoranda for management and clients.
	Provide insights and recommendations to senior management.
	Support investor relations activities and client engagements.
	Collaborate with internal teams and external stakeholders on transaction execution.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field.
	3-5 years of experience in capital markets, investment banking, asset management, or financial advisory.
	Master&#39;s degree or professional certification is an added advantage.
	Professional qualifications such as ACA, CFA, CIS, or ACCA are highly desirable.
	Strong knowledge of the Nigerian and global capital markets.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@elvaridah.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Capital Market Analyst Job at Elvaridah




        
    

        
        At Elvaridah, we emphasize on the critical matters and prospects of organizations such as business growth, strategy, planning, marketing, structure, and people management across different industries. We help companies focus on the growth of their businesses while aligning costs with strategy. With our all-inclusive approach and expertise, we capture values across the boundaries of any organization. Elvaridah focuses on optimizing all the different parts of a business as a unit.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Capital Market Analyst

Job Location: Lagos (Hybrid)
Department: Investment & Capital Markets
Reports To: Head, Capital Markets
Employment Type: Full-Time
     

            
    



Job Summary


	The Capital Market Analyst will be responsible for conducting market research, analyzing investment opportunities, monitoring capital market trends, and providing data-driven insights to support investment decisions and capital raising activities.
	The role requires strong analytical capabilities, financial modelling expertise, and a deep understanding of Nigeria's capital markets and regulatory environment.


Key Responsibilities
Market Research & Analysis:


	Conduct comprehensive research and analysis of capital market trends, economic indicators, and industry developments.
	Monitor equity, debt, and alternative investment markets and provide timely market intelligence.
	Analyze financial statements, company performance, and investment opportunities.
	Prepare market reports, investment recommendations, and sector analyses.


Financial Modelling & Valuation:


	Develop financial models for company valuations, capital raising transactions, and investment analysis.
	Conduct business valuations using DCF, comparable companies, and precedent transaction methodologies.
	Evaluate Initial Public Offerings (IPOs), bond issuances, rights issues, and other capital market transactions.
	Support due diligence processes for investment and fundraising activities.


Investment Advisory & Portfolio Support:


	Assist in developing investment strategies and recommendations.
	Monitor portfolio performance and provide periodic performance reports.
	Conduct risk assessments and scenario analyses.
	Support transaction execution and capital market deals.


Regulatory & Compliance Monitoring:


	Stay updated on capital market regulations, policies, and compliance requirements.
	Ensure adherence to guidelines issued by regulatory authorities and market operators.
	Support the preparation of regulatory filings and transaction documentation.


Reporting & Stakeholder Engagement:


	Prepare presentations and investment memoranda for management and clients.
	Provide insights and recommendations to senior management.
	Support investor relations activities and client engagements.
	Collaborate with internal teams and external stakeholders on transaction execution.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor's Degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field.
	3-5 years of experience in capital markets, investment banking, asset management, or financial advisory.
	Master's degree or professional certification is an added advantage.
	Professional qualifications such as ACA, CFA, CIS, or ACCA are highly desirable.
	Strong knowledge of the Nigerian and global capital markets.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",

  "title": "Full-Stack Software Engineer (Cloud, DevOps)",
  "description": "Innov8 Hub is an Innovation Startup driven organisation with a mandate to groom generations of INNOVATORS, INVENTORS, and RESEARCHERS across the board. Innov8 is a focal point for Innovation Startup Incubation, Technology Transfer, Knowledge and Skill Impartation, Prototype Development, and Fabrication. We strategically aid organizations and individuals seeking to transform their ideas into inventions, inventions into solutions, and solutions into enterprises.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Full-Stack Software Engineer (Cloud, DevOps)

Job Location: Lugbe, Abuja (On-Site)
Job Type: Full Time/Permanent

Job Description


	We are looking for a versatile and experienced Full-Stack Software Engineer with strong expertise in cloud infrastructure, DevOps, and AWS.
	This role requires a well-rounded engineer capable of building scalable applications while managing and optimizing the underlying infrastructure that supports them.


Requirements
Proven experience as a Full-Stack Engineer, with strong skills in:


	Front-end: React, Vue, Angular, or similar frameworks
	Back-end: Node.js, Python, Java, or similar languages
	Databases: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, DynamoDB
	Strong knowledge of AWS cloud services and architecture.
	Hands-on experience with DevOps tools (Docker, Kubernetes, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, etc.).
	Experience with Linux server administration.
	Strong scripting skills (Python, Bash, PowerShell).
	Ability to troubleshoot issues across the full stack&mdash;frontend, backend, and cloud
	Strong problem-solving and teamwork skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV and Cover Letter to: hr@innov8hub.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Full-Stack Software Engineer (Cloud, DevOps) Job at Innov8 Hub




        
    

        
        Innov8 Hub is an Innovation Startup driven organisation with a mandate to groom generations of INNOVATORS, INVENTORS, and RESEARCHERS across the board. Innov8 is a focal point for Innovation Startup Incubation, Technology Transfer, Knowledge and Skill Impartation, Prototype Development, and Fabrication. We strategically aid organizations and individuals seeking to transform their ideas into inventions, inventions into solutions, and solutions into enterprises.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Full-Stack Software Engineer (Cloud, DevOps)

Job Location: Lugbe, Abuja (On-Site)
Job Type: Full Time/Permanent

Job Description
     

            
    




	We are looking for a versatile and experienced Full-Stack Software Engineer with strong expertise in cloud infrastructure, DevOps, and AWS.
	This role requires a well-rounded engineer capable of building scalable applications while managing and optimizing the underlying infrastructure that supports them.


Requirements
Proven experience as a Full-Stack Engineer, with strong skills in:


	Front-end: React, Vue, Angular, or similar frameworks
	Back-end: Node.js, Python, Java, or similar languages
	Databases: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, DynamoDB
	Strong knowledge of AWS cloud services and architecture.
	Hands-on experience with DevOps tools (Docker, Kubernetes, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, etc.).
	Experience with Linux server administration.
	Strong scripting skills (Python, Bash, PowerShell).
	Ability to troubleshoot issues across the full stack—frontend, backend, and cloud
	Strong problem-solving and teamwork skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Electrical Engineer",
  "description": "Sinoma Nigeria Company Limited is the subsidiary of CBMI Construction Co., Ltd, whose senior company is CNBM Group. Sinoma Nigeria is mainly responsible for project management and services in the Federal Republic of Nigeria and West Africa. Scope of business includes but not limited to fabricating, Industry Constructional Engineering, Civil Constructional Engineering, etc.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Electrical Engineer

Job Location: Katampe, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

&nbsp;

Description


	We are seeking qualified and motivated Electrical Engineers to join its growing team in Abuja.
	The successful candidates will be responsible for designing, developing, testing, and maintaining electrical systems and equipment while ensuring compliance with applicable safety standards and engineering practices.
	The role offers an opportunity to contribute to innovative projects and work within a dynamic, collaborative environment.


Key Responsibilities
Design and Development:


	Design electrical systems, components, and products for commercial, industrial, and domestic applications.
	Develop engineering solutions that meet project specifications and operational requirements.
	Prepare electrical layouts, designs, and technical documentation.


Testing and Evaluation:


	Develop testing procedures for electrical systems and equipment.
	Evaluate system performance for safety, reliability, and efficiency.
	Identify defects and recommend corrective actions where necessary.


Drafting and Engineering Documentation:


	Produce technical drawings, schematics, and electrical layouts using CAD and engineering software.
	Prepare reports, calculations, specifications, and design documents.
	Maintain accurate engineering records and documentation.


Project Management:


	Assist in estimating project costs and resource requirements.
	Monitor project schedules and ensure timely completion of assigned tasks.
	Support budget control and efficient project execution.


Collaboration and Coordination:


	Work closely with mechanical, civil, and other engineering teams.
	Coordinate with contractors, consultants, suppliers, and project stakeholders.
	Participate in project meetings and provide technical input.


Compliance and Safety:


	Ensure all electrical designs and installations comply with applicable industry standards and safety regulations.
	Adhere to relevant electrical codes, including NEC and IEC standards where applicable.
	Promote safe work practices and maintain compliance with company safety policies.


Essential Qualifications
Education:


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Electrical Engineering, Electronics Engineering, or a closely related STEM discipline.
	Professional Certification
	Engineer-in-Training (EIT) certification is an advantage.
	Professional Engineer (PE) license is preferred for advanced roles and added responsibility.


Technical Skills:
Candidates should possess knowledge or experience in:


	Autocad
	Solid works
	Revit
	Electrical system design and analysis
	Technical drawing and schematic preparation
	Electrical testing and troubleshooting
	Engineering calculations and reporting


Soft Skills:
The ideal candidate should demonstrate:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
	Good organizational and time-management skills.
	Ability to work independently and within multidisciplinary teams.


Working Conditions:


	Office and site-based assignments.
	Occasional travel to project locations may be required.
	Compliance with company health, safety, and environmental policies is mandatory.


Benefits


	Competitive salary package; N175,000 - N200,000 monthly.
	Opportunities for professional growth and career development.
	Exposure to major engineering and construction projects.
	Dynamic and collaborative work environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV and relevant credentials to: sinomanigeriacompanyltd@gmail.com using &quot;Application for Electrical Engineer Position&quot; as the subject of the email.

Note


	Equal Opportunity Statement
	SINOMA Nigeria Company Limited is committed to providing equal employment opportunities and encourages qualified candidates to apply.

Electrical Engineer Job at Sinoma Nigeria Company Limited




        
    

        
        Sinoma Nigeria Company Limited is the subsidiary of CBMI Construction Co., Ltd, whose senior company is CNBM Group. Sinoma Nigeria is mainly responsible for project management and services in the Federal Republic of Nigeria and West Africa. Scope of business includes but not limited to fabricating, Industry Constructional Engineering, Civil Constructional Engineering, etc.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Electrical Engineer

Job Location: Katampe, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

 

Description
     

            
    




	We are seeking qualified and motivated Electrical Engineers to join its growing team in Abuja.
	The successful candidates will be responsible for designing, developing, testing, and maintaining electrical systems and equipment while ensuring compliance with applicable safety standards and engineering practices.
	The role offers an opportunity to contribute to innovative projects and work within a dynamic, collaborative environment.


Key Responsibilities
Design and Development:


	Design electrical systems, components, and products for commercial, industrial, and domestic applications.
	Develop engineering solutions that meet project specifications and operational requirements.
	Prepare electrical layouts, designs, and technical documentation.


Testing and Evaluation:


	Develop testing procedures for electrical systems and equipment.
	Evaluate system performance for safety, reliability, and efficiency.
	Identify defects and recommend corrective actions where necessary.


Drafting and Engineering Documentation:


	Produce technical drawings, schematics, and electrical layouts using CAD and engineering software.
	Prepare reports, calculations, specifications, and design documents.
	Maintain accurate engineering records and documentation.


Project Management:


	Assist in estimating project costs and resource requirements.
	Monitor project schedules and ensure timely completion of assigned tasks.
	Support budget control and efficient project execution.


Collaboration and Coordination:


	Work closely with mechanical, civil, and other engineering teams.
	Coordinate with contractors, consultants, suppliers, and project stakeholders.
	Participate in project meetings and provide technical input.


Compliance and Safety:


	Ensure all electrical designs and installations comply with applicable industry standards and safety regulations.
	Adhere to relevant electrical codes, including NEC and IEC standards where applicable.
	Promote safe work practices and maintain compliance with company safety policies.


Essential Qualifications
Education:


	Bachelor's Degree in Electrical Engineering, Electronics Engineering, or a closely related STEM discipline.
	Professional Certification
	Engineer-in-Training (EIT) certification is an advantage.
	Professional Engineer (PE) license is preferred for advanced roles and added responsibility.


Technical Skills:
Candidates should possess knowledge or experience in:


	Autocad
	Solid works
	Revit
	Electrical system design and analysis
	Technical drawing and schematic preparation
	Electrical testing and troubleshooting
	Engineering calculations and reporting


Soft Skills:
The ideal candidate should demonstrate:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
	Good organizational and time-management skills.
	Ability to work independently and within multidisciplinary teams.


Working Conditions:


	Office and site-based assignments.
	Occasional travel to project locations may be required.
	Compliance with company health, safety, and environmental policies is mandatory.


Benefits


	Competitive salary package; N175,000 - N200,000 monthly.
	Opportunities for professional growth and career development.
	Exposure to major engineering and construction projects.
	Dynamic and collaborative work environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV and relevant credentials to: [email protected] using "Application for Electrical Engineer Position" as the subject of the email.

Note


	Equal Opportunity Statement
	SINOMA Nigeria Company Limited is committed to providing equal employment opportunities and encourages qualified candidates to apply.
    

    

        Click here to Apply
